summary:
The Median Empire (c. 678–550 BC) was the first Iranian empire — an alliance of Mede tribes that rose in the highlands of northwestern Iran to become a major power, eventually conquering the Neo-Assyrian Empire in alliance with Babylon and establishing dominance over a vast territory from eastern Anatolia to Central Asia. Founded by Deioces (according to Herodotus) and expanded by Cyaxares, who reorganized the army on the Assyrian model, the Medes were the dominant power in the Near East for a brief century.

Cyaxares allied with the Neo-Babylonian Nabopolassar to destroy Nineveh (612 BC) and the Assyrian empire, then fought a five-year war with Lydia that ended in a stalemate cemented by the solar eclipse of 585 BC (possibly predicted by [[event:1938|Thales of Miletus]]) — the famous 'battle halted by an eclipse.' The Median Empire was overthrown by Cyrus the Great of Persia around 550 BC — Cyrus's grandfather (by some traditions) was the Median king Astyages. The Medes were absorbed into the Achaemenid Persian Empire rather than conquered in the usual sense; Persian and Median aristocracies intermarried and the Greeks frequently referred to the Persians as 'Medes' indiscriminately.
